description:
Standing on Lower Queens Reservoir boat launch, when I heard bzzrrrt call of EAME. 

I looked down as 6 or so meadowlarks took flight, most or all of which seemed to have a lot of white on the tail. I found a juv meadowlark perched in good view. The head stripes were dark brown contrasting with pale yellow-tinged beige. The eyeline was prominent and equal in darkness to lateral crown stripe. The auricular patch was pale, but not white. The malar was whitish and uniformly colored. The throat was pallid yellow. The folded tail showed brown central tail feathers with dark brown cross barring. The upper tail coverts were barred similarly but with prominent dark brown shaft streak on each feather. 

The birds flew again. A thin rattle, higher and "tinnier" than that of WEME was heard as well as a bzzzrrrt coming from at least two birds. This time goodly look at tail of 4 birds in flight, all of which appeared to have about 3/4 of tail white when tail fanned moderately and perhaps as much as 5/6 white when fully fanned upon landing. I found an adult in good view facing mostly away but body angled enough that face was in good view, especially when bird turned head towards me. This bird was in view for 1 minute or so. Dark head stripes were blackish with eyeline equal in color to central crown stripes. Pale stripes were white, excepting yellow lores. Auricular patch was white, pretty much same as white malar. Throat was yellow, with sharp demarcation between yellow and white. Scapulars /upper back were black with rust suffusion. Folded tail was similar to that of juv: brown central rects with dark brown cross barring. Upper tail coverts were as in juv. Birds flew again, and went too far to be seen well. Pursuit was unsuccessful
